evals.prompt:
Shape this intent brief:

OBJECTIVE: Build a real-time notification system for our SaaS platform (React frontend, Node.js backend, PostgreSQL). Users need in-app notifications, email digests, and webhook integrations for third-party tools.

NON-GOALS: Mobile push notifications, SMS alerts, notification analytics dashboard.

CONSTRAINTS: Must work with existing auth system (JWT). Max 50ms latency for in-app notifications. Budget for third-party services capped at $200/month.

ACCEPTANCE: Notifications delivered within 50ms in-app. Email digests batch and send daily. Webhook retry with exponential backoff. User preferences respected per channel.
evals.expected_output: Full 5-step shape workflow. Multiple HOW dimensions identified (architecture, data model, delivery mechanism, testing at minimum). Expert consultation for irreversible dimensions. Integration step checks cross-dimension tensions and runs pre-mortem. Final shape document emitted with all required sections including semantic checksum and first step.
expectations: Step 1 (Extract) identifies at least 3 shaping dimensions from the brief
Step 1 searches the codebase for existing patterns and prior art before presenting findings
Step 1 addresses dichotomy of control — identifies what's within the user's control vs externalities
Step 2 (Scope) classifies domain type and reversibility — stated as brief lines, not asked unless ambiguous
Step 3 (Shape loop) consults expert perspectives for at least the Type 1 (irreversible) dimensions
Step 3 presents distinct approach options with tradeoffs for each dimension
Step 3 gives lighter treatment to Type 2 (reversible) dimensions than Type 1 — fewer options, less analysis, or skipping expert consult
Step 4 (Integrate) checks cross-dimension tensions between chosen approaches
Step 4 runs a pre-mortem — imagines the shaped solution failed 6 months later
Step 4 lists second-order effects — what each choice prevents in 6-12 months
Step 5 (Emit) produces a shape document with sections: Approach, Codebase findings, Resolved tensions, Risks, First step
Step 5 shape document includes a semantic checksum — one sentence explaining why the shape exists
Step 5 First step section names one atomic action that produces a visible artifact
No reasoning technique names appear in the shape document output (no 'inversion', 'Musashi', 'obstacle-as-path', 'dichotomy of control')

evals.prompt: Shape this: Our Express.js API returns errors inconsistently — some endpoints throw, some return { error }, some use HTTP status codes only. We want to standardize on a single error response format. The API has about 40 endpoints across 3 route files.
evals.expected_output: Recognizes this as a single-dimension task (error format choice) and collapses to Step 1 → Step 5. Does not force the user through full dimension selection and integration checks for a straightforward standardization decision.
expectations: Recognizes this as a single-dimension task and collapses the workflow (Step 1 → Step 5)
Does NOT present a multiSelect dimension picker — the single dimension is self-evident
Does NOT run a full integration step with cross-dimension tensions (only one dimension exists)
Does NOT ask the user about domain type or reversibility for this low-stakes task
Still searches for existing error handling patterns or prior art (codebase, docs, or web)
Still presents implementation choices with distinct approaches and tradeoffs (e.g., RFC 7807 vs custom format vs GraphQL-style errors)
Shape document is proportionally brief — not padded to fill a full multi-dimension template
Shape document includes a First step section with one atomic action

evals.prompt:
Shape this intent brief:

OBJECTIVE: Integrate Stripe payment processing into our e-commerce platform. Handle subscriptions (monthly/annual), one-time purchases, refunds, and failed payment recovery. Must be PCI-DSS compliant.

NON-GOALS: Cryptocurrency payments, invoice generation, multi-currency support.

CONSTRAINTS: Stripe as sole payment processor. All card data handled by Stripe.js — no server-side card handling. Must pass PCI SAQ-A compliance. Existing user model has no payment fields.

ACCEPTANCE: Subscriptions created and billed correctly. Failed payments retried 3 times over 7 days. Refunds processed within 24 hours. No raw card data touches our servers.
evals.expected_output: Infers high-stakes domain (financial, compliance). Applies deeper analysis per dimension. Checks that broken states can't be expressed in types. Failure paths identified and addressed. Second-order effects analysis is substantive. Pre-mortem catches adoption and integration risks specific to payment systems.
expectations: Step 2 (Scope) infers high-stakes domain type — states classification as a brief line without asking
Dimensions include data model and security/compliance at minimum
Step 3 applies deeper analysis for high-stakes dimensions (not exploratory-light treatment)
Step 3 reasoning checks that broken payment states can't be expressed in types (e.g., partial charge without order)
Step 3 identifies failure paths (failed charges, declined cards, webhook failures) and addresses how they're handled — not silently swallowed
Step 4 second-order effects analysis is substantive — names specific futures closed off by choices
Step 4 pre-mortem identifies payment-specific risks (not generic software risks)
Shape document Risks section addresses compliance and financial integrity concerns
Expert consultation includes Stripe-specific technical content (not just generic architecture advice with payment-themed role names)
Shape document includes a semantic checksum — one sentence explaining why the shape exists
Shape document First step section names one atomic action that produces a visible artifact

evals.prompt: Shape this: We need to add real-time collaborative editing to our document editor. The backend team is migrating from REST to gRPC but the timeline is TBD — could be 2 weeks or 2 months. The infrastructure team controls the WebSocket gateway and we need their approval to add new channels. Our PM wants this shipped in 3 weeks but the design team hasn't finalized the cursor/presence UI yet.
evals.expected_output: Identifies mixed control boundaries. Separates externalities (gRPC migration timeline, WebSocket gateway approval, design team deliverables) from shapeable decisions. Documents external dependencies as constraints, not choices to be shaped.
expectations: Step 1 (Extract) addresses dichotomy of control — identifies what the user can actually decide vs external dependencies
Identifies externalities: gRPC migration timeline, WebSocket gateway approval, design team UI deliverables
Externalities are documented as constraints, NOT presented as dimensions to shape
Shapeable dimensions focus only on what the user controls (e.g., CRDT vs OT algorithm, local state model, conflict resolution strategy)
Does not shape the WebSocket gateway decision (owned by infrastructure team)
Does not make visual design decisions for cursor/presence UI (colors, sizes, animations, layout) — owned by design team
Step 4 pre-mortem considers what happens if external dependencies slip or change
Shape document separates 'what we decided' from 'what we depend on'
Shape document includes a First step section with one atomic action
